key_insight: CPTabKAN combines physiologically grouped EEG concepts with polynomial interaction modeling and a KAN-based tabular classifier to improve interpretable MCI detection.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a clinically scalable, interpretable feature-learning framework for screening cognitive decline from overnight EEG data.
method: Organize 1,379 EEG-derived features into 10 domain-motivated concept groups
Apply degree-2 polynomial expansion to capture first- and second-order cross-concept interactions
Use a Fourier-parameterized TabKAN classifier for nonlinear tabular prediction
evidence_signal: On 372 subjects with 10-fold cross-validation, CPTabKAN-Second Order reached weighted F1 = 0.9038, outperforming GradientBoosting by 5.65 points and retaining gains under SMOTE balancing.
watch_item: Performance and interpretability depend on carefully defined concept groupings, and the approach should be validated on independent cohorts before clinical deployment.
paper_title: Interpretable Concept-Guided Polynomial Tabular Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for EEG-Based Mild Cognitive Impairment Detection
